title: CPS Admin Application
body_text:
CPS Admin Application
1	Client Centre and CPS Admin Introduction
1.1	Overview of the Client Centre and CPS Admin
The Client Centre is a digital portal that provides secured access to Central 1âs applications and to information and news about our products and services. All users must be set up with a Client Centre user ID and must register a username and password at the first login.
There are currently two user management applications running parallel: the Central Portal System (CPS) Admin and User Management. Eventually, User Management will replace CPS Admin, so CPS Admin Security Officers are encouraged to use the User Management application. Client Centre Users are displayed in both systems and changes can be made in either CPS Admin or User Management, with some exceptions. Specifically, you canât manage tokens in CPS Admin, and you canât manage realms in User Management. CPS Admin Security Officers can also delete and modify other CPS Admin Security Officers via the User Management application, with some exceptions. This Part provides procedures for CPS Admin Security Officers who are still using CPS Admin.
On CPS Admin, users are set up with authorization to access specific applications. Users access these applications via the Client Centre. Since some applications also have a separate user management component, users may require set-up on both CPS Admin and the individual application.
Most applications on the Client Centre are accessed via password login. However, a 2-step security token is also required to access some applications (e.g., CPS Admin and User Management) or to access functions within some applications (e.g., to approve a wire within the Wires application). See the 2-Step Security Part for more information.
There are two user types: CPS Admin Security Officers and Client Centre Users. Both types of users access the Client Centre to log in to applications. CPS Admin Security Officers are also responsible for creating, modifying, and deleting Client Centre Users. Another CPS Admin Security Officer must authorize all additions, modifications, and deletions.
